name: Protocol Complexity Burden Scorer
description: Quantify operational complexity and site burden from your protocol before study activation.
longDescription: Protocols are getting more complex. Phase III oncology protocols now average 263 procedures per patient and 3.6 million data points per study. The Protocol Complexity Burden Scorer reads your protocol and quantifies the operational load across six dimensions. In under 30 seconds, your team sees exactly where the burden is heaviest.
builtFor: Study startup managers, site directors, PIs, feasibility teams
howItWorks: Upload a protocol PDF. Flo State extracts every visit, procedure, eligibility criterion, and data requirement. It scores operational complexity across 6 dimensions on a 1-10 scale.
timeSaved: Replaces 4-8 hours of manual protocol analysis per study.
exampleUseCase: A CRO study startup manager receives three new protocols. She uploads all three to the Scorer and shares the scorecard with the medical monitor, recommending consolidating visits before site selection.

name: Protocol-to-Site-Operations Generator
description: Auto-generate site operational packages, schedules, and document checklists from a protocol.
longDescription: Every new study starts the same way. A coordinator receives a 200-page protocol and spends 40 to 80 hours manually extracting the information they need to run the study. The Generator reads the protocol once and produces the complete operational package. Five deliverables, ready for review.
builtFor: Clinical research coordinators, study startup managers, CRAs
howItWorks: Upload a protocol PDF. Flo State reads every section and generates five operational deliverables: visit schedule table, document checklist, role-based training requirements, source document worksheet, and site burden estimate.
timeSaved: 40-80 hours per study, per site. Across a 50-site study, that is 2,000-4,000 hours returned to the system.
exampleUseCase: A new CRC is assigned her first oncology study. Instead of spending her first two weeks manually building study binders, she uploads the protocol. Within a minute she has her visit schedule and knows exactly which regulatory documents to collect.

name: eISF-eTMF Reconciliation Bot
description: Cross-reference site and sponsor documents to identify mismatches automatically. In 10 seconds.
longDescription: CRAs spend 3 to 7 hours per site, per visit, cross-checking site documents against the sponsor's Trial Master File. Version mismatches, missing filings, expired credentials, naming inconsistencies-these are not clinical problems. They are filing problems. The Reconciliation Bot compares every document and generates a severity-rated discrepancy report.
builtFor: CRAs, TMF managers, quality assurance teams, site regulatory specialists
howItWorks: Flo State compares document metadata across both systems. Nine reconciliation rules run simultaneously, covering version mismatches, missing documents, naming inconsistencies, and filing location errors.
timeSaved: 3-7 hours per site per monitoring visit. For a CRA monitoring 10 sites monthly, that is 30-70 hours returned.
exampleUseCase: A CRA prepares for a routine monitoring visit. She runs the Reconciliation Bot before driving to the site. In 10 seconds, she sees three critical findings and contacts the site before the visit to request updates, cutting the visit time in half.

name: AI Regulatory Document Factory
description: Three specialized agents preparing your regulatory startup package in parallel.
longDescription: Regulatory document preparation is the single longest phase of site activation. It takes weeks because every document is prepared independently. The Regulatory Document Factory is a multi-agent system handling 1572 auto-population, delegation gap analysis, and CV/license expiration monitoring-and they talk to each other.
builtFor: Regulatory specialists, coordinators, study startup managers, site directors
howItWorks: Three sub-agents activate: 1572 Populator, Delegation Analyzer, and CV/License Monitor. An orchestration layer detects cross-document dependencies.
timeSaved: Reduces regulatory preparation from 3-6 weeks to same-day review.
exampleUseCase: A site regulatory specialist clicks 'Prepare Startup Package'. Three agents activate. The 1572 agent populates the form and detects a new sub-investigator. It triggers the delegation agent, which confirms training is current but protocol-specific training is missing.

name: Digital Trial Rehearsal
description: Rehearse your trial before going live. Find the bottlenecks before they find you.
longDescription: Every other industry rehearses before going live. Clinical trials spend millions activating sites with no simulation. Predict where things will break before you start. The Rehearsal models your trial at a specific site, running 500 statistical simulations using historical site performance.
builtFor: Study startup managers, clinical operations directors, site directors
howItWorks: Flo State runs a Monte Carlo simulation sampling from the site's historical distribution for each activation phase, multiplying by protocol complexity.
timeSaved: Prevents 2-8 weeks of avoidable delay per site by identifying bottlenecks before they happen.
exampleUseCase: A project manager runs the Rehearsal for multiple candidates. The academic center shows a high probability of contracting delays, while a dedicated site shows a clean activation path. She prioritizes the dedicated site.

name: Virtual SMO Layer
description: Your coordinator's AI-powered morning briefing. Every study. Every task. Every alert.
longDescription: Coordinators manage 7+ studies simultaneously, with 20+ systems and no unified view. The Virtual SMO Layer runs before your coordinator opens Flo State in the morning, curating a prioritized view of everything that needs attention across every study.
builtFor: Clinical research coordinators, site directors, research managers
howItWorks: Flo State aggregates data across all active studies for a specific coordinator: documents, credentials, schedules, sponsor queries, and deadlines. It curates a top priorities dashboard automatically.
timeSaved: 2-3 hours per coordinator per day. Over 5,000 hours/year for a typical site.
exampleUseCase: Maria opens Flo State at 8:00 AM. Her dashboard shows: 1) a monitoring visit today, fully pre-staged; 2) an expiring certification; 3) an AI-drafted reply to a sponsor query. Her morning prep is done.

name: Oncology Study Startup Kit
description: Everything a coordinator needs to launch an oncology study, in one workspace.
longDescription: Oncology trials have immense operational load, requiring weeks of learning for new coordinators. This workspace comes pre-loaded with comprehensive reference documents, RECIST, and NCI guidelines.
builtFor: Coordinators new to oncology, study startup managers
howItWorks: Install the workspace. It arrives pre-loaded with all required NCI CTCAE and FDA guidelines. Two key AI skills (Protocol Reader, Reg Calendar) are automatically enabled to cross-reference.
timeSaved: Hours per question. Accelerates junior coordinator onboarding by weeks.
exampleUseCase: A CRC asks 'What CTCAE grade is persistent nausea requiring IV hydration?' Flo State utilizes the locally embedded knowledge repository to provide an immediate citation.

name: Protocol Reader
description: Extracts structured metadata, schedules, and criteria directly from protocol PDFs.
longDescription: Turns any protocol PDF into structured, queryable data. Clinical trial protocols are dense, unstructured documents spanning 100-300 pages. Reader extracts structured data including visits, procedures per visit, eligibility criteria, study arms, and design parameters.
howItWorks: Accepts Protocol PDFs. Produces Structured JSON mapping with defined keys readable by other Flo State agents.
exampleUseCase: Used as a backend capability for the Protocol-to-Ops Generator to seamlessly map data.

name: CRA Monitoring Visit Prep
description: Walk into every visit knowing exactly what to look for.
longDescription: CRAs prepare for monitoring visits by reviewing site documents. This template creates a monitoring-focused AI workspace that understands ICH E6(R3) monitoring requirements.
howItWorks: Loaded with 4 knowledge documents and 2 pre-enabled skills (Document Comparator, Regulatory Calendar) to generate visit-specific checklists.
timeSaved: Standardized, intelligent visit preparation reduces missed findings and shortens visit time.
exampleUseCase: Generate a monitoring checklist for a routine visit at an oncology site.

name: Site Feasibility Response Assistant
description: Answer feasibility questionnaires in minutes, not days.
longDescription: Sites receive feasibility questionnaires for every study they consider. A single questionnaire takes 4-6 hours. This assistant drafts responses based on your actual site data.
builtFor: Coordinators, site business development leads, PIs responding to sponsor inquiries
howItWorks: Uses 3 knowledge documents and 2 pre-enabled skills (Protocol Reader, Clinical Terminology Translator) to assist with feasibility answers.
timeSaved: Turns a half-day burden into a 30-minute workflow.
exampleUseCase: How should I answer this question about our IRB turnaround time?

name: Amendment Impact Assessment
description: See the full cascade of every protocol change before it reaches your site.
longDescription: Protocol amendments are inevitable. The average Phase III protocol is amended 2.3 times. This template creates a workspace that compares original protocol sections against amended sections and generates a comprehensive impact report.
builtFor: Coordinators, regulatory specialists, study startup managers, CRAs
howItWorks: Contains 3 knowledge documents and 3 pre-enabled skills (Protocol Reader, Document Comparator, Regulatory Calendar) to review protocol changes.
timeSaved: Turns a multi-week scoping exercise into a same-day impact report.
exampleUseCase: Upload original and amended protocol. Show me what changed.

name: Regulatory Calendar
description: Never miss a regulatory deadline again.
longDescription: IRB renewals, license expirations. Every study has dozens of regulatory deadlines. This skill surfaces them by severity: overdue, due within 30 days, due within 90 days.
howItWorks: Scans your active studies and staff credentials, identifies every regulatory deadline. Produces Calendar events with due dates and severity.
timeSaved: Automates the manual tracking of hundreds of deadlines.
exampleUseCase: Expiration of PI's medical license triggers an alert.

name: Document Comparator
description: See exactly what changed between two versions of any document.
longDescription: Protocol amendments, ICF revisions, delegation log updates. Document Comparator identifies every difference between two document versions.
builtFor: CRAs, regulatory specialists, coordinators, TMF managers
howItWorks: Document Comparator identifies every difference between two document versions. Each change is categorized by type and severity.
exampleUseCase: Spotting a changed dosage limit in a 200 page amendment.

name: Clinical Terminology Translator
description: Turn protocol language into plain-English instructions for any role.
longDescription: Protocols are written by medical writers. Clinical Terminology Translator converts protocol and regulatory language into plain-English instructions calibrated for specific site staff roles.
builtFor: Coordinators, research nurses, data entry staff, training managers
howItWorks: Feed it a dense eligibility criterion and specify 'for CRC' and it produces clear, actionable screening instructions.
timeSaved: Eliminates confusion and operational errors during complex procedures.
exampleUseCase: Clarifying an adverse event grading definition for a specific CRC.

name: Audit Trail Generator
description: GxP-ready documentation of every AI-assisted action.
longDescription: Clinical trials operate under GCP regulations that require complete documentation of every action. Audit Trail Generator logs every AI-assisted action.
builtFor: Quality managers, regulatory specialists, site directors, anyone working in a GxP environment
howItWorks: Runs in the background during any Flo State session, logging inputs provided, outputs generated, user review decisions, and timestamps.
exampleUseCase: Generating an audit trail that can be included in the Trial Master File or presented during inspections.
